blocks.label: Application operations
blocks.content:
Module 1:
Introduction to ITIL
-> ITSM ITIL and its history

when company or bank fail to manage there IT infra strucutre and service .
eg let say you visit atm and transtion completed but u didn't get cash out. so this kind of failure comes due to operation failure.

blocks.label: ITSM
blocks.content:
Information technology and service management.

Defination: ITSM is set of organizational capablities to provide the value to the customer throught services.

What is Service:  Service is a mean of delivering the value to the customer by faciliating the outcomes that the customer want to achieve without the ownership of specfic cost and risk.

blocks.label: ITIL
blocks.content:
Information technology and infrastructure library.
Defination : ITIL is a set of pratices for IT service management that focus on alignment the IT service with the needs of business.

It is framework which help below:
-> Increase the productivity
-> Lower IT cost
-> Improving service

History of ITIL:
Orginally developed in 1980's by UK Goverment agency  - CCTA( forerunner of OGC)
* Central computer telecommunication agency.
* Office of goverment commerce

Since Jan 2014 ITIL is owned and managed by axelos

Version:
1980's - Infrastructure management grew into more that 30 books cover specfic aspect of IT service and provision (version 1)
2001 - Intial version was reviesed and  replace by 9 books  focus on service delivery and support.(version 2)
2007 - 5 core book focus on service lifecycle approach (version 3)
2011 - Improved clarity and consistency (version 4)

comapny used ITIL frame work
1) Microsoft
2) Atos
3) shell
4) IBM

blocks.label: Type of services
blocks.content:
1) Core Services
2) Enabling Services
3) Enhancing Services

-> Core Services: 
 It is a service that must be there for any value to be delivered
-> Enabling Service
It is a service that must be place for a core service to be delivered.
-> Enhancing Services:
Service that are added to  the core services to make it more enticing to the customer are called Enhancing Services

blocks.label: Service Asset
blocks.content:
Service asset consist  of capablities and resources that the organizational use to deliver the value in the form of service

Type of service provider:
Internal service provider- for every domain they have there own IT team manage internally, and also they can be internally centralized.
Extranal service provider - act as intermediate between customer and company which provide service. let say example tcs proviode service to db,passport kendr etc.

blocks.label: Key param of ITIL
blocks.content:
Process - a structural set of activies  designed to accomplish  a specify objective
Function - team or group of people  and tools they use to  carry out the process and acitivty
role - Played by individual to within function  when performing process.

children.name: Strategy Management and Service Portfolio Management

content.title: Strategy Management and Service Portfolio Management

blocks.label: Strategy Management Activities
blocks.content:
4 Major activities in statege management
1) Market definition - defining who the customer are of IT service;
2) Develop offering - Identifying service to be offered customer
3) Develop Strategic asset -Identifying the asset that may use asbuilding block for creation of IT services
4) Prepare for execution - Preparing the IT organization to carry out the servie stategy sucessfully.

blocks.label: Service Strategy process
blocks.content:
1) Strategy Management  for IT service ( what is strategy)
2) Service protfolio management ( what  do we deliver)
3) Demand managment  ( what is requested)
4) Financial management ( whom i can bill to)
5) Bussiness relationship manangemnt ( is good relationship with customer)

blocks.label: Four Ps form of Strategy
blocks.content:
1) Perspective - vision and direction of organization 
2) Postion - describes how the service providers intent to compete with other service provider in the market
3) Plan - Describe how service provider will transition from current state to their desire state.
4) Pattern - Describes on going repeatable action  that a service provider will have to preform consistently in order to continue to meet its strategic object.

blocks.label: Strategy management sub-process
blocks.content:
1) Strategic assestment
2) Strategy generation
3) strategy execution
4) contual serve improvement

blocks.label: Service Portfolio managment
blocks.content:
Defination - Service portfolio  describe the servics of a providerin in terms of values to the business.

Propose of Service portfoliio managment:
The purpose of service portfolio management  (SPM) is to  create manage improve  a service portfolio contain detail design package for each IT services

SPM helps to 
1) Decide the  services to offer and to whom.
2) Maintain a definatvie portfolio of all services provided.
3) Control under what condition services are offered and at what level of investment.
4) Track the investment in services througout their lifecycle

Parts of SPM 
1) Service pipeline
2) Service catalogue
3) Retired Services

Key activity of SPM 
 Define -> Analyze->  Approve -> charter ->Define
